page: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or hardware GPU accelerators, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 124975.29 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17772.0. Single-core performance is moderate, as indicated by a Geekbench single-core score of 1677.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1909.29. The server exhibits top-tier performance in specific vector and cryptographic operations, scoring 106991.36 in PassMark CPU extended instructions and 112635.39 in PassMark CPU encryption, aided by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 337.73 GB/sec for large block read-only operations.

This bare-metal configuration is suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, and database operations, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 30121.63 and a Redis SET throughput of 108.92 million operations per second. It also supports moderate machine learning tasks, processing 196.66 tokens per second for 7B model prompts. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. The high density of dedicated physical cores and memory provides a balanced resource profile for virtualization, container orchestration, and enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This instance does not contain local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with top-tier results in cryptographic and extended instruction tests, alongside strong database and Redis operations. Single-core performance remains average. The server is suited for enterprise applications, multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and container orchestration where dedicated physical hardware is required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory bandwidth, and 128 vCPUs for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
Strong multi-core, database, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Bare-metal deployment without local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for enterprise applications, database operations, and virtualization
